title: lomatium foeniculaceum subsp. macdougalii
intro: Lomatium foeniculaceum subsp. macdougalii, also known as MacDougal's Lomatium or Lomatium, is a perennial plant that belongs to the carrot family (Apiaceae) and is native to the southwestern United States. The plant has been used in traditional herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antibacterial compounds. The health benefits of Lomatium include its ability to support immune function, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms associated with colds and flu. Lomatium is often prepared as a tincture, tea, or salve, and is also used in capsule or powder form to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
